steps.title: Substitute the shared value
steps.content:
The intersection point is $(q,16)$, so $y=16$ in both equations:

$$-x-16w=-361$$
$$2x-16w=50$$

steps.title: Eliminate the repeated term
steps.content:
Subtract the first substituted equation from the second:

$$(2x-16w)-(-x-16w)=50-(-361)$$
$$3x=411$$
$$x=137$$

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content:
Use $x=137$ in one substituted equation:

$$-137-16w=-361$$
$$-16w=-224$$
$$w=14$$

The answer is 14.
